title: DTD
text: DTD A DTD is a Main Development Object that represents a Document Type Definition that defines element declarations and a default mapping between the XML elements and Component Entities and Fields. It can be used to define the rules for the structure of an XML stream that is exchanged between components. Characteristics Characteristics Object type code: dtd Main Development Object? Yes Part of: — Contains: Property Generalization: Main Development Object Specializations: — Compilation Output: compiled DTD, readable DTD URI format: dtd: EntityName Specialization DTD specializes Main Development Object in the following ways: It has properties. It contains no development objects or code containers. Properties DTD Name Description Definition Default Mapping File Name External Reference Comments Related Links Uniface-Compliant DTDs A Document Type Definition (DTD) defines rules for the structure of an XML stream. In Uniface, a DTD is a development object that includes DTD definitions and a default mapping between the XML elements defined by the DTD and Uniface component fields and entities. It can be used to define the rules for the structure of an XML stream that is exchanged between components. Working with DTDs Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
